title: I Tried Shopping Animal-Based at Sam’s Club

chunks.title: Introduction and Grass-Fed Beef
chunks.summary: The video begins with a grocery haul at Sam's Club, focusing on 100% grass-fed beef burgers, which the speaker prefers for their taste and nutritional value. The distinction between fully grass-fed and grain-finished beef is emphasized, discussing the nutritional superiority of the former.
chunks.transcript: all right guys Sam's Club grocery haul video thanks for coming along with me meet first here they've actually got grass-fed burgers here yep look at this grass-fed beef burgers at Sam's Club 100% grass-fed beef 8020 I like the fattiest Burgers I can get they just taste better that way these are $4.80 a pound less than $5 a pound for grass-fed beef it's amazing so when I look at stuff that's grass-fed I'm always thinking is it 100% grass-fed some manufacturers are writing grass-fed on their burgers now or grass-fed on their meat but we don't know if it's fully grass-fed from the beginning of its life to the end of its life what we want is meat that's fully grass-fed 100% grass-fed and not finished on grains that's better I mean it's not the end of the world heat grain finish meat I just prefer 100% grass-fed beef no preservatives I can look at the label only ingredient grass-fed beef on the label I love that when you buy a burger

chunks.title: Plant-Based Protein Comparison
chunks.summary: The speaker compares the protein content in grass-fed beef to that in plant-based meat alternatives, arguing that animal protein is more bioavailable than plant protein. He critiques the marketing of plant-based products that claim to have high protein content while highlighting the many ingredients and fillers in these alternatives.
chunks.transcript: look at that distinction with something like this beyond meat plant-based patties they try to pretend there's protein in there 20 G of plant protein but we know that that plant protein is not nearly as bioavailable as the protein and animal foods that protein and grass-fed beef is really twice as available when we do studies as this plant protein so that 20 grams of protein and plant-based protein is less than probably 10 grams of actual real usable protein by the human body and look even on the plant-based Meats they're advertising no soil and no gluten this is like plant-based movement look I love vegans I I think their intention is great but the plant-based movement is collapsing from the inside they're realizing things like soy and gluten are horrible and they're trying to advertise we're plant-based but we don't even contain soy and gluten which is crazy when you think about how many plant-based foods do contain soy and gluten I was at another grocery store here in Austin the other day and I got this ingredient list of a Korean barbecue ribs that were plant-based there were 95 things on this ingredient list but look at this remember one ingredient on the beef look at how many ingredients on the plant-based Burgers including seed oils like canola oil and a bunch of synthetic fake vitamins so that they can actually make the nutrition label look like it Stacks up to that single ingredient of beef crazy

chunks.title: Raw Dairy and Cheese
chunks.summary: The speaker discusses the benefits of raw dairy, particularly raw cheese, mentioning its association with lower rates of asthma and allergies. He encourages viewers to read labels to distinguish raw from pasteurized products and warns against additives like kappa carrageenan found in some dairy items.
chunks.transcript: if you're at Sam's Club and you're on a budget you can get even cheaper meat I'm going to get the grass-fed beef but look at this you can get 80% lean Angus beef patties here for $367 a pound so that's even cheaper than the grass-fed beef I think it's worth the extra dollar for the grass-fed beef let's go get some cheese I think it's pretty cool that even at these big stores like Sam's Club or Costco you can get raw cheese this is parmesano regano cheese if it says parmesano regano it's a raw cheese it's a really high quality cheese I'm going to buy that cheese made from pasteurized milk is okay but I'm a fan of raw milk raw cheese raw dairy in general raw milk has been shown consistently to be associated with lower rates of asthma eczema allergies and kids compared to pasteurized milk so I think there's probably a prote Ive role for the whey protein in raw dairy and I try and eat as much of my Dairy raw as I can I like sheep cheese like Mano but you can just see on the label it'll say pasteurized sheep's milk and when something is raw it'll say raw milk so you can just read the label of your cheese and know if it's pasturized or raw the only one here that's raw is that parano regano that one has to be raw to be called prano reano and as you're shopping in Dairy looking at things that are thick like creams you got to read the labels you can see this is a Mexican style cream but on the label it contains kagenin that's a problem that's a long polysaccharide derived from algae that we know is irritating for the gut both animal and human models show that so I

chunks.title: Choosing Quality Meats
chunks.summary: The speaker outlines his preference for grass-fed beef over chicken, explaining the negative effects of corn and soy feed on chicken quality. He briefly mentions the potential risks associated with fish consumption, emphasizing the need for sourcing wild-caught options.
chunks.transcript: that are trying to be thick potentially Greek yogurts creamers that kind of stuff might have kerogen hiding in it so you got to read the label I'm going to get some pickles yes pickles can be animal- based cucumbers are a fruit and they're fermented so that's going to get rid of any of the defense chemicals in there now these pickles contain garlic if you've got gut issues they might irritate your gut but I'm not going to eat the garlic in here it's mostly just in here in whole garlic cubes to flavor it so I'm going to go a little wild today get some pickles and you know enjoy it here we got the chicken again I'm not going to go for this chicken because chickens are fed corn and soy they kind of accumulate that linolic acid in their fat and I think if I have a choice and I can get grass fed beef that's going to be the highest quality meat in this store I'm just going to go with that grass-fed beef but if you want to eat chicken okay I wouldn't make it the only type of meat in your diet it's not my favorite but it's not bad it's still an animal- based food isn't going to contain defense chemicals like vegetables the same can be said for fish let's go talk about fish I don't eat a lot of fish for a lot of reasons in general fish is going to be much higher in pfas Forever chemicals microplastics heavy metals depending on the source bigger fish like tuna more heavy metals even salmon you got to be careful with so if you're looking for salmon you want to make sure it's a Pacific wild salmon anything that says Atlantic salmon is a farm rais salmon and it's going to have coloring in it let's look at this Sakai salmon here it is wild caught Gulf of Alaska that's going to be a better

chunks.title: Fruits and Vegetables: Did Someone Say Oxalates?
chunks.summary: The discussion shifts to fruits and vegetables, specifically focusing on high-oxalate foods like spinach, while suggesting caution with non-organic produce due to pesticides. The speaker shares concerns over the potential negative health impacts of oxalates found in various common foods.

chunks.title: Avoiding Seed Oils and Processed Foods
chunks.summary: The speaker warns against the consumption of seed oils, labeling them a significant threat to health and chronic illness. He discusses the harmful effects of grains and other processed foods, advocating for a diet that minimizes these foods.

chunks.title: Importance of Carbohydrates
chunks.summary: The video highlights the need for carbohydrates in an athletic diet, where the speaker mentions sources like maple syrup and honey. The speaker posits that carbohydrates improve sleep and workout recovery while being cautious about added ingredients to these products.
chunks.transcript: phytic acid again the chemical that's going to chelate minerals and Rob you of minerals even though oats contain some zinc we know that the phytic acid in the oats is going to prevent you from absorbing that zinc and it's going to prevent you from absorbing some of the zinc in any of the other Foods you're eating so I'm skipping the oats I think that's a horrible breakfast in fact there's a study that shows in adolescent girls who are overweight that eating a beef and eggr protein breakfast was much better from a satiety perspective less hunger led to less snacking and easier weight loss and change their neurochemistry compared to a cereal based breakfast like pancakes or oatmeal so there's evidence that beef and eggs for breakfast way better than Kodiak power cakes or pancakes or oatmeal that stuff is just that's subpar on the carbohydrate front I can get some organic maple syrup when I'm looking at maple syrup I just want to make sure there's no caramel color added it's just pure maple syrup you'll see caramel color added to things like sodas and in animal studies caramel color is associated with all sorts of problems I believe increased certain cancers so you don't want to eat things with hidden caramel color in them they've got some honey here too again I like having carbohydrates in my diet I feel like it improves my sleep helps me recover better from workouts improves strength during workouts they don't have any organic honey but they've got a local raw and unfiltered Texas honey so pretty good if you're curious about my macros or you want to know how much carbohydrates protein and fat I might recommend you can check out my website Paul saladino md. there's a free calculator there based on your weight how active you are that'll give you a sense of what I do in terms of my macros carbohydrates protein and fat breakdown

chunks.title: Conclusion: Nutrient-Rich Animal-Based Diet
chunks.summary: The video concludes with a reiteration of the benefits of an animal-based diet, emphasizing nutrient density while minimizing toxins from foods. The speaker urges viewers to consider healthier food choices and invites them to subscribe for more content.
chunks.transcript: into what you might actually put on your plate nothing's perfect but pretty darn good even in a place like this a big box store Costco Sam's Club BJs you can affordably eat an animal based diet you can avoid a majority of the pitfalls in mainstream diets and I think an animal based diet organs meat fruit honey raw dairy these are the most nutrient rich foods on the planet they're really going to help humans Thrive and I've seen so many people get better from all kinds of chronic illness when they cut out things like seed oils high fructose corn syrup kerogen and Grains a lot of people even get better when they cut out vegetables if you're thriving you don't have any gut issues or autoimmune issues you can probably eat some vegetables especially cooked vegetables I choose not to eat them because I feel better without them it's kind of the idea behind animal based diet what are the most nutrient-rich foods that you can eat while also getting the least amount of toxins in your diet I'm going to go look and see if there's any frozen fruit because we didn't get much fresh fruit if you find this video helpful please like subscribe share with one of your friends and stay tuned for more of this
